skill.name: ki-register-use-case-documenter
skill.description: Document AI use cases for EU AI Act governance, inspect code or interview users, then validate and submit confirmed manifests to KI-Register.
skill.summary: Create KI-Register AI use case records from code inspection or stakeholder interviews.
skill.author: Egonso

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported critical and high patterns, but review found no prompt injection or confirmed malicious intent. The real risks are legitimate but sensitive actions: local CLI execution, environment API key handling, project inspection, and authenticated submission to KI-Register. Weak cryptography and path traversal alerts appear to be false positives from descriptive text and relative documentation paths.

medium_findings.title: Local CLI Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run a local Node-based studio-agent CLI. This is purpose-aligned, but it can execute project-local code if used in an untrusted repository.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and repeated. The risk depends on the local CLI implementation, so the concern is real but not evidence of malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated External Submission
medium_findings.description: The workflow submits a manifest to an external KI-Register endpoint using environment credentials. It requires explicit confirmation, but the action can disclose governance data if misused.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint, API key variable, register id, and submit command are directly documented. Human confirmation and local validation reduce the severity.

medium_findings.title: Project Inspection May Capture Sensitive Details
medium_findings.description: The skill asks agents to inspect routes, config, prompts, workflows, scripts, tests, and docs before creating records. This can collect sensitive architecture or process details.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspection scope is broad and explicitly includes sensitive project materials. The skill also requires evidence-backed drafts and review, so the risk is contextual.

low_findings.title: Environment Secret Handling Uses Protective Guidance
low_findings.description: The static env_access findings are true, but the skill tells users to keep API keys in environment variables or secret stores and not write them into generated files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variables are visible in the instructions, and the same file includes explicit secret handling safeguards. No evidence shows credential exfiltration beyond intended authenticated submission.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded KI-Register URLs Are Purpose-Aligned
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to KI-Register onboarding and submission locations. This is expected for the skill purpose, but users should understand that submission leaves the local environment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are clear first-party KI-Register endpoints for account setup and submission. No unrelated or covert destination was found.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Alerts Dismissed
low_findings.description: No evidence of weak cryptographic algorithms was found at the reported locations. The flagged lines are descriptions or interface metadata, not cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no hashing, cipher, random number, or cryptographic API usage. The reported lines contain prose headings or descriptions.

low_findings.title: Static Path Traversal Alerts Dismissed
low_findings.description: The path traversal alerts refer to relative documentation paths used for portability instructions. No executable file access or user-controlled traversal was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relative paths are static references to schema, sample, and documentation files. There is no code that reads arbitrary paths from user input.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents Node CLI commands for onboarding, capture, validation, interview, and submission. Agents should only run them in trusted repositories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is explicit. The risk is environmental because the referenced local CLI is outside these scanned files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credentialed Network Submission Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines environment credentials with a submit command and a remote endpoint. This is intended behavior, but it is still sensitive.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The API key, register id, endpoint, and submission command are adjacent. The instructions also require explicit confirmation before submitting.

dangerous_patterns.title: Repository Data Capture Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs agents to inspect project materials and convert findings into documentation artifacts. This can include sensitive operational context.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented capture scope and output paths are specific. Risk depends on what the target repository contains.

content.user_title: Document AI Use Cases for KI-Register
content.value_statement: AI governance teams need clear records for applications, processes, and workflows. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to capture evidence, validate manifests, and submit confirmed records.
actual_capabilities: Guides documentation for AI applications, processes, and workflows.
Supports codebase inspection when local project evidence is available.
Supports stakeholder interviews when the workflow is not visible in files.
Creates manifest and README outputs under a workflow documentation folder.
Directs local validation with the studio-agent CLI before submission.
Requires human confirmation before submitting to KI-Register.
limitations: It does not provide legal advice or conformity assessments.
It depends on Node and the local Agent Kit CLI for command workflows.
Submission requires valid KI-Register API credentials and a register id.
It cannot verify interview answers without supporting evidence.

use_cases.title: Prepare governance records
use_cases.description: Create structured AI use case documentation for internal review and audit preparation.

use_cases.title: Document product workflows
use_cases.description: Inspect application routes, prompts, systems, and controls to draft evidence-backed workflow records.

use_cases.title: Capture supplier information
use_cases.description: Interview stakeholders and collect missing details for procurement or vendor review.

prompt_templates.title: Start a guided interview
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner capture when no repository evidence is available.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to interview me about one AI workflow. Ask one question at a time and create a draft KI-Register use case.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect a project
prompt_templates.scenario: Evidence-based capture from a local repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to inspect this project for AI features. Create a manifest draft with evidence-backed facts and list any unknowns.

prompt_templates.title: Validate an existing draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Review an already prepared workflow folder.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review the existing workflow documentation. Validate the manifest, find missing required fields, and suggest precise fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare confirmed submission
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow before sending data to KI-Register.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to summarize the completed manifest for approval. Submit it only after I explicitly confirm the target register and endpoint.

output_examples.input: Interview me about our customer support chatbot and prepare a use case record.
output_examples.output: A concise interview plan, followed by a draft use case summary with purpose, owner, systems, data categories, human checkpoints, risks, controls, and missing facts.

output_examples.input: Inspect this repository and document any AI workflow you can support with evidence.
output_examples.output: A workflow documentation folder containing a machine-readable manifest and a human-readable README summary for review.

output_examples.input: Validate this AI workflow before submission to KI-Register.
output_examples.output: A validation summary that lists blockers, missing fields, evidence gaps, and the exact confirmation needed before submission.

best_practices: Review the generated README before submitting any manifest.
Store API keys only in environment variables or a secret manager.
Mark uncertain facts as missing instead of guessing.
anti_patterns: Do not submit inferred manifests without explicit human confirmation.
Do not place API keys in manifests, README files, logs, or screenshots.
Do not treat the output as legal advice or final risk classification.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates structured AI use case documentation, including a manifest and a human-readable review summary.

faq.question: Can it inspect my codebase?
faq.answer: Yes. It can inspect local project files for evidence of AI workflows and record only supported facts.

faq.question: Can it work without code access?
faq.answer: Yes. It can interview a stakeholder and ask targeted questions to collect required use case details.

faq.question: Does it submit data automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill says submission should happen only after local validation and explicit human confirmation.

faq.question: What credentials are needed for submission?
faq.answer: Submission needs a KI-Register API key and register id stored outside generated documentation.

faq.question: Is this a legal compliance decision tool?
faq.answer: No. It supports structured documentation and review, but it is not legal advice or a conformity assessment.
